science.title: Meteor sighting lights up Texas sky, triggers sonic booms
science.description: A dramatic celestial event unfolded across parts of Texas on March 21, as a bright fireball streaked across the evening sky, drawing widespread attention from residents and sparking scientific interest, National Aeronautics and Space Administration Centre (NASA) reported.

science.title: Houston meteor explosion today: 35,000 mph fireball unleashes 26 tons of TNT energy, NASA confirms Texas boom
science.description: A loud boom echoed across Texas on Saturday, March 21. Residents initially feared an explosion. Officials confirmed the sound was from a meteor. NASA stated the object broke apart mid-air. This fragmentation created a pressure wave causing the booms heard. Fragments may have reached the ground. Another fireball was spotted over Ohio earlier this week.

science.title: Houston, did you hear the sonic BOOM? A meteor is the culprit!

science.title: NASA’s Hubble accidentally caught a comet breaking apart in real time
science.description: In an incredibly lucky cosmic accident, NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured a comet breaking apart in real time—something astronomers have long tried and failed to observe. The comet, C/2025 K1 (ATLAS), wasn’t even the original target, but when researchers pivoted to it, they unknowingly caught it mid-disintegration into multiple pieces.
